quiz.title: Unit 12 Quiz 2, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Gas Pressure Units and Conversions
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Pressure Units, Unit Conversions, Atmospheric Pressure, Manometers, Absolute vs Gauge Pressure

questions.question: The SI unit of pressure is the:
questions.explanation: The Pascal (Pa) is the SI unit of pressure, defined as 1 Newton per square meter. Atmosphere, torr, and bar are commonly used but are not SI units.

questions.question: Standard atmospheric pressure is approximately:
questions.explanation: Standard atmospheric pressure (1 atm) is defined as exactly 101,325 Pa.

questions.question: Select all that apply: 1 atmosphere is equal to:
questions.explanation: 1 atm = 760 torr = 760 mmHg = 101.325 kPa. 101325 kPa would be 101,325,000 Pa, which is 1000 times too large.

questions.question: Which pressure unit is defined as exactly 100,000 Pa?
questions.explanation: 1 bar is defined as exactly 100,000 Pa (10⁵ Pa). 1 atm is 101,325 Pa, 1 torr is about 133.3 Pa, and 1 psi is about 6,895 Pa.

questions.question: A pressure of 2.5 atm is equivalent to:
questions.explanation: 2.5 atm × 760 torr/atm = 1900 torr exactly. 2.5 atm × 101.325 kPa/atm = 253 kPa (not 2500 kPa). 2.5 atm × 1.01325 bar/atm = 2.53 bar (not 25 bar). 2.5 atm × 14.7 psi/atm = 36.8 psi (not 370 psi).

questions.question: The pressure at sea level is approximately:
questions.explanation: At sea level, standard atmospheric pressure is defined as 1 atm (101.325 kPa or 760 mmHg).

questions.question: Pressure decreases with altitude because:
options: Less air mass above exerts force
questions.explanation: At higher altitudes, there is less air mass above, so the weight of the air column decreases, resulting in lower pressure.

questions.question: 1 mmHg is defined as the pressure exerted by:
questions.explanation: 1 mmHg is the pressure exerted by a 1 mm column of mercury at 0°C under standard gravity. This unit is also called a torr.

questions.question: If a barometer reads 750 mmHg, the pressure in atm is:
A tire pressure of 30 psi gauge pressure equals absolute pressure of:
questions.explanation: Absolute pressure = gauge pressure + atmospheric pressure (≈14.7 psi). 30 psi + 14.7 psi = 44.7 psi ≈ 45 psi.

questions.question: In an open-end manometer, if the mercury level is higher on the gas side:
options: Gas pressure is less than atmospheric
questions.explanation: If the mercury level is higher on the gas side, the atmospheric pressure is pushing the mercury up, meaning gas pressure is less than atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: The unit 'millibar' (mbar) is commonly used in:
questions.explanation: Millibars are commonly used in meteorology for atmospheric pressure measurements. 1 bar = 1000 mbar, and standard pressure is about 1013 mbar.

questions.question: A pressure reading of 98 kPa is slightly:
questions.explanation: Standard atmospheric pressure is 101.325 kPa. 98 kPa is about 3.3 kPa less, so it is below standard atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: 5 microbar is equal to:
questions.explanation: 1 bar = 10⁵ Pa, so 1 microbar = 10⁻⁶ bar = 10⁻⁶ × 10⁵ Pa = 0.1 Pa. Therefore, 5 microbar = 5 × 0.1 Pa = 0.5 Pa.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered gas pressure units and conversions! 🧪⚗️
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review pressure unit conversions and absolute vs gauge pressure.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the relationships between different pressure units.
scoreMessages.0: Please review pressure units, conversions, and manometer principles.
